title: Three Drivers Receive Immediate Roadside Prohibitions in Nelson
content:
The Nelson Police Department continues to conduct proactive impaired driving enforcement throughout the community.
Between May 29 and May 31, 2026, Nelson Police officers conducted three separate impaired driving investigations that resulted in Immediate Roadside Prohibitions (IRPs).
• A
17-year-old male Nelson resident
was stopped in the 100 block of Vernon Street during the early morning hours of May 31 after officers observed a vehicle being operated without headlights. The driver provided two breath samples that resulted in "FAIL" readings on approved screening devices. The driver received a
90-day driving prohibition
and the vehicle was impounded for
30 days
.
• An
18-year-old female South Slocan resident
was stopped on Hall Street during the early morning hours of May 30 after officers observed a traffic violation. The driver provided a breath sample that resulted in a "WARN" reading. The driver received a
3-day driving prohibition
and the vehicle was impounded for
3 days
.
• A
71-year-old male Winlaw resident
was stopped on Silica Street on May 29 following a complaint from a member of the public. The driver provided two breath samples that both resulted in "FAIL" readings. The driver received a
90-day driving prohibition
and the vehicle was impounded for
30 days
.
The Nelson Police Department reminds motorists that impaired driving remains one of the leading causes of serious injury and fatal collisions in British Columbia. Officers will continue conducting roadside enforcement and screening throughout the year to help keep our roads safe.
Anyone who suspects an impaired driver is encouraged to call 911 if the behaviour presents an immediate danger or report the information to local police as soon as it is safe to do so.
Update to January 19, 2026 Media Release File NP2026-125
Health Canada laboratory analysis has confirmed that substances seized during a January 11, 2026 traffic stop in Nelson included cocaine, MDMA, LSD, ketamine, and benzodiazepines.
The investigation involved a 29-year-old male resident of Slocan, BC, who was arrested following a traffic stop conducted as part of ongoing impaired driving enforcement. Officers subsequently seized significant quantities of controlled substances, drug trafficking paraphernalia, and more than $4,000 in Canadian currency.
As part of the investigation, the vehicle involved was seized under the Civil Forfeiture Office (CFO) process.
The investigation remains before the courts. Charges related to Possession for the Purpose of Trafficking and impaired driving have been recommended.
Repeat Property Offender Facing Additional Charges
The Nelson Police Department has laid charges against Daniel DINUNZIO, a 36-year-old Nelson resident, following an investigation into a break and enter at the Kootenay Co-op.
On April 11, 2026, police received a report that an individual had unlawfully entered the business during the early morning hours and stolen merchandise. Surveillance footage captured the suspect gaining entry to the store before leaving with several items.
The suspect was identified as Daniel DINUNZIO by investigators and witnesses familiar with him through previous interactions.
At the time of the offence, DINUNZIO was subject to court-ordered conditions prohibiting him from attending several businesses in Nelson, including the Kootenay Co-op.
Police have laid charges of:
• Break and Enter
Additional charges related to breaches of court-ordered conditions remain under review.
DINUNZIO is currently in custody on unrelated matters. An unendorsed warrant has been issued in relation to these offences and will be executed while he remains in custody.
DINUNZIO is a designated Chronic Property Offender Intervention Initiative (CPOII) offender
Between May 24 and May 30, 2026, the Nelson Police Department responded to 179 calls for service within the City of Nelson. Calls included proactive patrol activities, traffic enforcement, property crime investigations, public safety concerns, and requests for police assistance from members of the community.
Media Contact:
Nelson Police Department

title: Reporting period: May 17 to May 23, 2026
content:
During the week of May 17 to May 23, 2026, Nelson Police Department responded to 144 calls for service. Calls included property-related files, suspicious persons or circumstances, traffic incidents, wellbeing checks, unwanted persons, thefts, bylaw matters, and requests to assist members of the public and partner agencies. Officers also responded to several higher-priority incidents, including domestic-related calls, mental health and wellbeing concerns, impaired driving, threats, and an assault file.
144 total calls for service were completed during the reporting period.
Police handled multiple property-related files, thefts, and mischief occurrences across the city.
Officers responded to a number of suspicious person and suspicious circumstance complaints.
Traffic enforcement and road-related responses included traffic incidents, a hit and run, and an impaired driving file.
Wellbeing checks, mental health-related calls required police attendance.
Members also assisted the public, other agencies, and emergency partners on a variety of calls.
Driver issued prohibition after collision in Nelson
NPD File – 2026-2654
Nelson Police responded to a motor vehicle incident in the 200 block of Innes Street on the morning of May 18, 2026.
Police were called after reports that a vehicle had been involved in a collision and then continued driving before striking a curb. Officers attended and spoke with witnesses at the scene.
During the investigation, officers formed grounds to demand breath samples from the driver, identified as a 27-year-old female from Nelson, using an approved screening device. One sample resulted in a “Warn.” The driver was issued a 3-day Immediate Roadside Prohibition, and the vehicle was impounded for 3 days. Violation tickets were also issued in relation to the incident.
No serious injuries were reported.
Nelson Police remind drivers that impaired driving and leaving the scene of a collision put everyone at risk. If you have been drinking, do not drive. Plan a safe ride home and report dangerous driving to police when it is safe to do so.
Driver issued 90-day prohibition following traffic stop near Nelson
NPD File -- 2026-2752
On the evening of May 22, 2026, Nelson Police conducted traffic enforcement in the area of Highway 3A and 6 near Lakeview Crescent.
During enforcement, an officer stopped a pickup truck after observing it travelling well above the posted speed limit in a 50 km/h zone.
During the traffic stop, the driver, identified as a 60-year-old male from Thrums, provided breath samples into an approved screening device, which resulted in a “Fail.” As a result, the driver was issued a 90-day Immediate Roadside Prohibition, and the vehicle was impounded for 30 days.
Motor Vehicle Theft Suspect Sentenced in File 2026-1758
Update: In relation to file 2026-1758, a theft of motor vehicle that occurred on April 5, 2026, the accused, Louis McIvenna, a 47-year-old man from Vernon, B.C., was charged with theft of motor vehicle contrary to section 333.1(1) of the Criminal Code and failing to comply with a release order contrary to section 145(5)(a) of the Criminal Code. He was sentenced to two years and three months in custody.
This weekly summary is intended to provide a general overview of police activity in Nelson. Information is preliminary and may change as investigations continue.
Media Contact:
Nelson Police Department

title: Weekly Media Release – Highlights (May 10–17, 2026)
content:
Nelson, B.C.
— The Nelson Police Department is providing a weekly summary of notable calls for service and enforcement actions from May 10–17, 2026.
“Our officers respond to a wide range of calls each week from community assistance and medical-related calls to traffic enforcement,” said Community Safety Officer Singh. “We’re asking everyone to do their part by making safe choices and contacting police right away if they suspect someone is driving impaired.”
Drug-impaired driving investigation – Gyro Park Road
On May 12, 2026, police responded to a report of Male occupants using cannabis in a parked vehicle in the parking lot at 25 Gyro Park Rd. Officers located unoccupied vehicle and observed drug paraphernalia inside, When the occupants returned and began to drive away, an officer conducted a traffic stop.
Police identified the driver as 21 years old male from Nelson at the scene. Based on witness information and observations, the officer formed
[JJ1]
a suspicion the driver had cannabis in his body while operating a motor vehicle and made a Standardized Field Sobriety Test (SFST) demand (The Standardized Field Sobriety Test (SFST) is a series of roadside tests used by police to help assess possible impairment. The SFST includes the Horizontal Gaze Nystagmus (eye test), Walk and Turn test, and One Leg Stand test). After the SFST, the officer formed the opinion the driver’s ability to drive was affected by a drug.
The driver was served with a 24-hour Notice of Driving Prohibition under section 215 of the
Motor Vehicle Act
. The driver’s licence and keys were seized, and the vehicle was impounded and towed from the scene.
“Impaired driving puts everyone at risk drivers, passengers, and pedestrians,” Community Safety Officer Singh added. “If you see a driver, you believe may be impaired, call 911.”
Motor vehicle incident – Collision with pole (mall parking lot)
On May 16, 2026, at approximately 10:45 a.m., police responded to a report of a vehicle collision with a pole in a mall parking lot. The driver reported he was checking for traffic behind him while moving through the lot and did not see the pole. The vehicle sustained extensive front-end damage and was towed. The driver had a minor scrape to his hand that was bandaged by Nelson Fire Rescue. Police had no suspicion of alcohol, speed, or impairment in this incident.
“Even low-speed collisions can cause injuries and significant damage,” said Community Safety Officer Singh. “Drivers are reminded to slow down in parking lots, keep distractions to a minimum, and take extra care when backing or maneuvering around fixed objects.”
Traffic enforcement – Stop sign violation and uninsured vehicle
On May 11, 2026, at approximately 8:32 p.m., an officer conducting traffic enforcement near Gyro Park witnessed a vehicle proceed through a stop sign at Gyro Park Rd and Morgan St without coming to a complete stop. Police stopped the vehicle and determined the vehicle was uninsured. The vehicle was towed and violation tickets were issued
.
“Traffic enforcement is about preventing collisions and keeping roads safe for everyone,” said Community Safety Officer Singh. “We’re reminding drivers to come to a complete stop at stop signs and to ensure their vehicle is properly insured before driving.”
Weekly call volume:
Nelson Police responded to 158 calls for service during this reporting period.
[JJ1]
a

title: Media release
content:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
During the week of May 3 to May 9, 2026, Nelson Police Department officers responded to 121 calls for service throughout the city.
Calls for service included suspicious occurrences, traffic complaints, collisions, mental health related incidents, property offences, assaults, disturbances, and public assistance calls.
90-Day Driving Prohibition Issued Following Traffic Stop
On May 3, 2026, at approximately 9:51 p.m., a Nelson Police Department officer conducted a traffic stop on Government Road after identifying a vehicle associated to a prohibited driver. Police confirmed the principal operator of the vehicle had no valid driver’s licence.
The driver, a 39-year-old male resident of Nelson, displayed signs consistent with alcohol consumption during the investigation and subsequently provided a “Fail” reading on an Approved Screening Device.
The driver was issued a 90-day Immediate Roadside Prohibition, and the vehicle was impounded for 30 days..
Nelson Police Department reminds motorists that impaired driving remains one of the leading causes of serious collisions and fatalities on BC roadways.
File: NP2026-2337
Police Investigating Suspected Drug Impaired Driving Following Collision
On May 4, 2026, at approximately 3:00 p.m., Nelson Police Department responded to a report of a collision on Perrier Road involving a pickup truck that struck a parked vehicle before leaving the scene. Witnesses later reported the same vehicle driving erratically through Nelson.
Officers later located the driver, a 58-year-old female resident of Nelson, at a residence on Highway 3A. Police observed the driver operating a second motor vehicle after arriving at the location.
During the investigation, officers observed signs consistent with drug impairment. The driver was issued a 24-hour driving prohibition and both vehicles involved were towed by police.
A blood demand was subsequently obtained as part of the ongoing Criminal Code investigation. The blood samples will be submitted for laboratory analysis, and criminal charges are being contemplated pending the results.
File: NP2026-2346
Nelson Police Department reminds the public to report suspicious activity and impaired driving behaviour when it is safe to do so.
Media Contact:
Nelson Police Department

title: Media Release
content:
On April 28, 2026, at approximately 10:47 a.m., the Nelson Police Department responded to a disturbance at a local accommodation on Highway 3A.
Staff reported a 24-year-old male was causing a disturbance and making concerning comments toward employees. Upon police arrival, the individual was located on the property in an escalated state.
The male was arrested for mischief. During the arrest, he resisted officers and assaulted two officers by kicking them. No serious injuries were reported.
The male was transported to Kootenay Lake Hospital for assessment. While at the hospital, the male bit a police officer, resulting in a minor injury.
The male was subsequently released on an undertaking with conditions to appear in court at a later date and was left in the care of hospital staff, as he was suffering a medical issue.
Driver prohibited following roadside investigation
On April 25, 2026, at approximately 8:40 p.m., Nelson Police officers conducted a traffic stop in the downtown area after observing a vehicle being driven erratically.
During the investigation, officers formed grounds that the driver, a 41-year-old male, had alcohol in their body and made a roadside breath demand. The driver failed to provide suitable samples despite multiple opportunities and was issued a 90-day Immediate Roadside Prohibition for refusing to provide a breath sample and the vehicle was impounded for 30 days.
Police respond to multiple calls for service
Between April 27 and May 2, 2026, Nelson Police Department officers responded to a variety of calls for service throughout the city.
Notable files include several reports of fraud over $5,000, a break and enter to a commercial property on Vernon Street, multiple thefts from vehicles, and incidents involving mischief under $5,000.
Officers responded to multiple motor vehicle collisions, including incidents involving injuries and significant property damage. Patrols also addressed public intoxication, disturbances, and bylaw-related complaints.
Police conducted several well-being checks and responded to mental health-related calls. These incidents were handled with the assistance of partner agencies where required.
A number of files remain under investigation.

title: Media release
content:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
The Nelson Police Department is advising the public of a recent theft from a vehicle and subsequent police investigation.
On April 18, 2026, at approximately 10:00 a.m., police received a report of a theft from a vehicle that had occurred overnight in the 600 block of Highway 3A. Officers attended and were able to recover a number of items associated with the incident, though some property remained outstanding.
Later that day, police received information that a financial card linked to the incident had been used at a local business. Officers attended, reviewed available surveillance footage, and identified a suspect.
Police subsequently located and arrested one individual in the downtown area in connection with the fraudulent use of the card. During the investigation, a second individual was also located and arrested for possession of property related to the incident.
Property connected to the investigation was recovered and returned to the owner.
Both individuals were later released on undertakings with conditions and are scheduled to appear in court later.
The investigation remains ongoing.
Police remind the public to secure their vehicles and avoid leaving valuables inside.
Update 25-3136
The Nelson Police Department is providing an update on a previously reported obstruction incident.
In June 2025, an individual fled from police during an interaction in a local park, resulting in an officer sustaining minor injuries during the pursuit. The individual was arrested and later charged.
On April 14, 2026,
Jason Ryan Moore
pled guilty
to a charge of Obstructing a Police Officer Section 129(a) of the Criminal Code
in court and was sentenced to 30 days in custody, along with a victim surcharge.
Weekly Update
During the week of April 12 to April 18, 2026, the Nelson Police Department responded to a total of 133 calls for service.
These calls included a range of incidents such as property-related files, unwanted persons, drug-related occurrences, liquor violations, thefts, and other public safety matters.
The volume and variety of calls highlight the ongoing work of frontline officers in responding to community needs and maintaining public safety throughout the City of Nelson.
Police remind residents to report suspicious activity and to take proactive steps to secure their property.

title: Nelson Police Remind Student Drivers of Laws, Fines, and Responsibilities Ahead of Graduation
content:
Nelson, BC
The Nelson Police Department is reminding student drivers to follow all rules under British Columbia’s
Motor Vehicle Act
and Graduated Licensing Program (GLP) as graduation celebrations approach.
In the lead-up to graduation day, Nelson Police will be conducting enhanced road checks and enforcement throughout the community. Officers will be ensuring compliance with “N” (Novice) and “L” (Learner) driver requirements, including proper display of signage and adherence to all licence conditions.
Recent enforcement has identified common violations, along with the following penalties:
Fail to display “N” or “L” sign
(
Motor Vehicle Act
)
Fine: approximately
$109
Fail to comply with GLP restrictions
Fine: approximately
$109
+
penalty points
Exceeding passenger restrictions (Novice drivers)
Fine: approximately
$109
+
penalty points
Learner driver without a qualified supervisor
Fine: approximately
$109
(driver responsible)
The
supervisor may also be ticketed
if they fail to properly supervise
Use of electronic device while driving
Fine:
$368
+
4 penalty points
Impaired driving (alcohol or drugs)
Immediate roadside prohibition,
vehicle impound
, significant fines, and possible criminal charges
Liability and Responsibility
Drivers are legally responsible for always complying with their licence conditions. For Learner (“L”) drivers, both the
driver and supervising adult
share responsibility for ensuring all conditions are followed.
Vehicle owners may also face consequences if they knowingly allow a driver to operate a vehicle contrary to licence restrictions or in an unsafe manner.
Police are strongly urging all students to plan ahead:
Do not drive impaired. Ensure you have a designated driver or safe transportation arranged.
Graduation is an important milestone, and the Nelson Police Department wants all students to celebrate safely and return home without incident.
“These laws are in place to protect new drivers and the public,”
said Nelson Police Department.
“We will be actively enforcing them to ensure everyone’s safety during graduation events.”
Enforcement efforts will continue throughout graduation activities.
Media Contact:
Nelson Police Department

title: Nelson Police Investigating “Door Kick Challenge” Incident on Davis Street
content:
Nelson, BC
The Nelson Police Department is investigating an incident involving youth participating in a so-called “door kick challenge” in the 800 block of Davis Street, which resulted in significant damage to a residential door.
This reckless behavior, often shared on social media, involves kicking or striking doors of homes and businesses. While it may be perceived as a prank, it can cause serious property damage, create fear for residents, and lead to criminal charges.
Police are reminding the public that this activity is
not harmless
and may result in the following criminal offences under the
Criminal Code of Canada
:
Mischief (Section 430)
Damaging or interfering with property can result in charges. Penalties may include fines, restitution (paying for damages), probation, or criminal records.
Mischief Under/Over $5,000
Charges depend on the extent of damage. More significant damage can lead to more serious consequences, including potential jail time.
Trespassing at Night (Section 177)
Entering or remaining on private property near a dwelling at night without lawful excuse is a criminal offence.
Causing a Disturbance (Section 175)
Behavior that disrupts the peace and safety of others may also result in charges.
In addition to criminal charges, youth involved may face
civil liability
, meaning they (and potentially their parents/guardians) could be responsible for covering the cost of repairs.
The Nelson Police Department is urging parents and guardians to speak with their children about the risks and consequences of participating in social media trends that involve illegal or dangerous behavior.
“What may seem like a joke can have serious legal and financial consequences,”
said Nelson Police Department.
“This type of activity can cause real fear for residents and will be treated seriously.”
Police are continuing to investigate and are asking anyone with information about this incident to contact the Nelson Police Department.
Media Contact:
Nelson Police Department

title: Police arrest suspect following residential break and enter in Nelson
content:
On April 1, 2026, at approximately 1:35 p.m., the Nelson Police Department received a report of an unknown male inside a residence in the 500 block of West Beasley Street.
Frontline officers attended and confirmed a break and enter had occurred. The suspect was located inside the residence and fled on foot when police arrived. Officers maintained containment and the suspect was taken into custody a short distance away without incident.
A 44-year-old male, Keith Fubey, who has no fixed address, was arrested in relation to the incident and held for a bail hearing.
No injuries were reported. The investigation remains ongoing.

Proactive patrol leads to quick recovery of stolen vehicle and arrests
On April 5, 2026, at approximately 10:43 p.m., the Nelson Police Department received a report of a stolen vehicle. The vehicle had been parked at the Ymir Road Park and Ride and was discovered missing when the owner returned.
At 11:01 p.m., while actively patrolling for the vehicle, officers located it parked in the 2800 block of Perrier Road. Police arrested two occupants at the scene: a male who was in the driver’s seat and a female passenger.
During a search incidental to the male’s arrest, officers located a wallet belonging to another individual, several vehicle keys from different makes of vehicles, and 3.63 grams of suspected methamphetamine. Further investigation revealed the male was bound by a court‑ordered release and was breaching three conditions, including a prohibition on occupying the driver’s seat of any motor vehicle.
The 20‑year‑old female, who has no criminal record, was later released from custody on an undertaking to appear in court at a later date.
The male, identified as 46‑year‑old Louis McIlvenna, was held in custody for a bail hearing. He has been charged with the following offences:
Theft of a Motor Vehicle — Section 333.1(1), Criminal Code
Possession of Stolen Property — Section 355(b), Criminal Code
Possession of a Controlled Substance — Section 4(1), Controlled Drugs and Substances Act
Three counts of Failing to Comply with a Release Order — Section 145(5)(a), Criminal Code
Mr. McIlvenna is scheduled to appear in court on April 8, 2026.
Inspector Jason Jewkes: “I’m proud of the work our officers did on this file. They spotted the stolen vehicle quickly, handled the arrest safely, and followed through with a solid investigation. It’s a great job by our members and a real example of the commitment they bring to keeping our community safe.”
The Nelson Police Department responded to approximately
117 calls for service
between March 29 and April 04, 2026, addressing a wide range of incidents across the city.

title: Weekly Policing Statistics (March 15 – March 21, 2026)
content:
During the period of March 15 to March 21, 2026, Nelson Police Department officers responded to 111 calls for service within the City of Nelson.
Calls for service included a variety of public safety and community assistance matters such as:
• Multiple assist public occurrences • Several check wellbeing and mental health-related calls • Reports of theft, mischief, fraud, and property-related incidents • Suspicious persons / circumstances and unwanted person complaints • Drug-related files and disturbances • Traffic incidents and one motor vehicle incident • A number of false alarms and abandoned 911 calls
Officers also conducted proactive patrols, assisted partner agencies, and continued ongoing investigations during this reporting period.
Nelson Police Department reminds the public to report suspicious activity and emergencies by calling 911 when immediate police attendance is required.

title: Nelson Police Department launches NHL STREET™
content:
The Nelson Police Department is inviting local youth to hit the pavement this spring with the launch of a new NHL STREET™ hockey program, designed to be fun, inclusive, and accessible to every child in the community.
The NHL STREET™ program is the National Hockey League’s official youth street hockey league, offering a neighbourhood-based model that gives kids the opportunity to learn and play the game.
“This program is about creating opportunities,” said Nelson Police Inspector Jason Jewkes, who is spearheading the initiative. “We want to introduce kids to hockey in a way that’s welcoming, affordable, and inclusive for everyone.”
The program will run on May 23, May 30, June 6, and June 13 at the Lions Park basketball court. Each session will include a 20-minute practice followed by a game consisting of two 20-minute halves, with games scheduled between 8:00 a.m. and 12:00 p.m.
The Nelson Police Department hopes the program will serve as an introduction to hockey for many young people, helping to build confidence, develop skills, and foster a lifelong connection to sport.
Registration is $25 and includes an official NHL STREET™ jersey. Families who may find the fee challenging can register their child at no cost, ensuring the program remains barrier-free. Community members who wish to support the initiative can also sponsor a player for $25. To register, visit
nelsonpolice.ca/NHL-Street
.
The initiative is supported by a $2,500 contribution from the Nelson Police Foundation, approved by the Nelson Police Board. The funding will be used to purchase equipment for ongoing use and to significantly reduce participation fees from approximately $100 to $25, making the program accessible to more families.
For more information about NHL STREET™ or to volunteer, contact Inspector Jason Jewkes at
jjewkes@nelsonpolice.ca
.

title: Media Release
content:
On March 9, 2026, Nelson Police responded to reports of a disturbance in the downtown area. Officers attended promptly, intervened, and safely brought the situation under control. A weapon was seized without injury to those involved or the public.
Subsequent review of available evidence determined no charges are being pursued, and the matter has been concluded. Police thank the community for their cooperation and timely reporting.
Nelson Police Department
Weekly Police Activity
Between March 8 and March 14, 2026, Nelson Police Department members responded to approximately 138 calls for service throughout the city. Incidents included thefts, disturbances, suspicious persons and circumstances, traffic-related occurrences, property complaints, fraud investigations, bylaw matters, and requests to assist members of the public and outside agencies.
As part of ongoing proactive policing efforts, frontline officers conducted regular patrols across the city. Community Safety Officer also carried out focused patrols in identified hotspot areas within the downtown core to enhance visibility, support enforcement, and promote community safety.

title: Media Release
content:
The
Nelson Police Department
removed an impaired driver from the road early Saturday morning following a traffic stop in the downtown area.
On
March 7, 2026
, at approximately
4:00 a.m.
, an officer conducting patrols observed a
black Subaru Impreza
rev its engine and make a U-turn at the intersection of
Hall Street and Vernon Street
in Nelson. Police conducted a traffic stop shortly afterward in a parking lot in the
300 block of Hall Street
.
During the investigation, the officer observed signs of alcohol impairment from the driver. The driver was required to provide breath samples using an Approved Screening Device. The driver provided
two samples
, both of which registered a
“Fail.”
As a result, the driver was issued a
90-day Immediate Roadside Prohibition (IRP)
and the vehicle was
impounded for 30 days
. The driver was also issued a
violation ticket for no insurance
under the Motor Vehicle Act. The vehicle was towed from the scene.
Nelson Police continue to remind motorists that impaired driving poses a significant risk to public safety. Anyone planning to consume alcohol should arrange for a safe ride home.
Nelson Police Department – Weekly Policing Activity
The Nelson Police Department responded to 157 calls for service between March 1 and March 7, 2026.
During this reporting period, officers responded to a variety of calls throughout the City of Nelson including property offences, suspicious persons and circumstances, disturbances, and requests for assistance from the public. Police also responded to reports of theft, fraud, harassment, threats, and drug-related complaints.
Several calls involved individuals causing disturbances or refusing to leave businesses or residences when requested. Officers attended and addressed a number of unwanted person complaints at various locations within the city.
Police also responded to multiple suspicious activity calls including suspicious persons and vehicles reported in residential and commercial areas. These reports were investigated by attending officers to determine if criminal activity had occurred.
Officers investigated several property-related incidents including thefts from businesses and other locations within the city. Police also received reports of fraud and other property complaints during the reporting period.
Throughout the week, officers helped members of the public on numerous occasions and assisted other emergency services including fire and ambulance. Police also responded to wellbeing checks and mental health-related calls where individuals required assistance.
Traffic enforcement and roadway safety also remained a focus for officers. Police responded to several traffic incidents including motor vehicle incidents and hazardous situations. One impaired driving investigation was conducted during the week.
“Nelson Police officers continue to respond to a wide range of calls for service while working to ensure the safety of our community,” said CSO Singh. “We encourage residents to report suspicious activity and remain aware of their surroundings so concerns can be addressed quickly.”
The Nelson Police Department encourages the public to report suspicious activity or criminal behaviour when it occurs. Anyone with information about criminal activity is asked to contact the Nelson Police Department through the non-emergency line.

Nelson Police Department – 250-354-3919
606 Stanley St, Nelson, BC

title: February 23, 2026
content:
Locked up for local athletes: Over a dozen local business celebrities will be ‘jailed’ by Nelson Police for Polar Plunge® fundraiser
Witness Nelson Police officers in action as local leaders, business owners, and community champions are 'arrested' and held until they reach their bail at the Jail & Bail Fundraiser taking place this Thursday. The funds raised will support the upcoming Special Olympics Polar Plunge® at 12pm on Sunday, March 8 at Lakeside Park.
Taking place on Thursday, February 26, this light-hearted event will see volunteers ‘arrested’ and locked in a mock jail cell until they raise their assigned bail amount. Their only way out? Calling on friends, family, and colleagues to donate in support of local athletes participating in events throughout the year.
“This event is all about coming together to support inclusivity, having some fun, and rallying behind a cause that matters,” says Nelson Police Constable Lisa Schmidtke. “Our ‘inmates’ are stepping up to be good sports and community champions, and we encourage everyone to help bail them out.”
Throughout the event, spectators can:
Watch live arrests from 12-1 pm throughout the downtown core
See the detainees at the temporary ‘jail’ at the Nelson Squash Club (330 Baker Street) from 1 pm onward, until they make bail
Donate on-site
A sneak peak of some of the jailbirds include Thor’s Pizza Mike, Kootenay Co-op’s General Manager Ashley Elliot, and Matt Walker from the Nelson Brewing Co, as well as many more surprises!
This is the seventh year that the Nelson Police Department has taken the plunge to support BC Special Olympics and athletes in our community.
About Special Olympics BC
Since 1980, Special Olympics BC, a registered charitable organization, has provided high-quality sports programs and competitions to meet the needs and interests of individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities.
These sports opportunities provide athletes with far more than the physical benefits of improved health and athletic ability. The participation in sports provides opportunities for athletes to develop social skills, cultivate friendships, strive for and achieve goals, and increase their self-esteem – ultimately enriching lives.
In addition to year-round sports programs, Special Olympics BC offers opportunities to compete at the regional, provincial, national and international level through a four-year competition cycle for summer and winter sports.

title: Media Release
content:
On February 10, 2026, at approximately 10:19 a.m., Nelson Police Department officers attended a residence in the 200 block of Victoria Street at the request of Nelson Fire Rescue following a report of smoke inside an apartment operating as a short-term rental.
Fire personnel located several adult males inside the residence who required assistance exiting, possibly due to drug intoxication. While exiting, one individual was observed operating a vehicle and was subsequently arrested for driving while prohibited. He was later released on an Appearance Notice for Nelson Provincial Court, a Notice of Vehicle Impoundment for 7 days and a 24-hour Driving Prohibition under Section 215 of the Motor Vehicle Act, as his ability to operate a motor vehicle was affected by a drug.
During a safety check of the apartment, officers located firearms, ammunition, suspected controlled substances, and other weapons in plain view. The items were seized for evidentiary purposes. One individual was briefly detained and later released. The investigation remains ongoing.
Between February 8 and February 14, 2026, Nelson Police responded to 153 calls for service.

Nelson Police Department – 250-354-3919

title: During the period of February 1 to February 7, 2026, Nelson Police Department officers responded to approximately
content:
159 calls for service
across the city while continuing proactive patrols and enforcement.
Call types included abandoned 911 calls, false alarms, disturbances, suspicious occurrences, unwanted persons, thefts, mischief, traffic incidents, and property-related files. Officers also conducted numerous wellbeing checks and assisted members of the public and partner agencies. Investigations were initiated into assaults, break and enters, drug-related occurrences, harassment, and other Criminal Code matters. One overdose and a mental health-related incident were also reported during the period.
Community Safety and Targeted Enforcement Program (C-STEP)
The funding for this initiative was provided by the Government of British Columbia’s Community Safety and Targeted Enforcement Program (C-STEP)
.
Project Footloose
was completed last week as part of C-STEP. Officers conducted an additional
96 hours of focused foot patrols
in the downtown core.
During the project, officers:
Arrested
one female
on an outstanding warrant
Issued
one Motor Vehicle Act violation ticket
Issued
three Liquor Control and Licensing Act violation tickets
Issued
seven Liquor Control and Licensing Act warnings
Assisted with calls for service including an
overdose, Mental Health Act incident, wellbeing checks, assaults, mischief, and protest-related incidents
Collaborated with community partners including
Nelson Mental Health, ANKORS, Stepping Stones, Salvation Army, and Victim Services
Community Feedback
An informal questionnaire was conducted with
43 downtown businesses
regarding increased foot patrol presence.
37 businesses
reported increased foot patrols are beneficial
3 businesses
reported they are not beneficial
3 businesses
were unsure
11 businesses
noticed an increase in police presence
15 businesses
reported a decrease in street disorder in recent months
4 businesses
reported an increase in street disorder
3 businesses
reported no change
7 businesses
reported police were responsive and helpful when assistance was required
Nelson Police Department will continue proactive patrols and community engagement initiatives to support safety in the downtown core.

title: Suspicious Activity – Vehicles and Residences
content:
Nelson Police Department received multiple reports of suspicious activity involving individuals checking vehicle and residential doors in various areas of the city. No forced entry or thefts have been reported at this time. Nelson Police are actively patrolling affected areas and continuing to monitor for suspicious behaviour.
The public is encouraged to report activity such as individuals attempting to open vehicle or house doors, looking into vehicles, lingering on private property without an apparent purpose, moving between properties while checking door handles, or attempting to conceal their actions when observed. When safe to do so, callers are asked to note clothing descriptions, direction of travel, and any vehicles associated with the activity. Residents are reminded to lock vehicles and doors, remove valuables, and report suspicious activity promptly.
“Calling police when something looks out of place helps us respond quickly and can prevent property crime,” said CSO Harmandeep Singh.
Recovered Vehicle
On January 25, 2026, Nelson Police responded to a report of a vehicle taken without permission. Police conducted follow-up inquiries and the vehicle was located and recovered later the same day without damage.
The owner declined to pursue charges and the matter was concluded. There is no ongoing risk to the public.
“This incident was resolved without damage and there is no ongoing concern for public safety,” said CSO Harmandeep Singh.
Calls for Service:
Members of the Nelson Police Department responded to 129 calls for service in the City of Nelson during
this reporting period

title: Media Release
content:
On January 28, 2026, at approximately 12:50am, officers were on patrol and observed a vehicle stopped with the brake lights on and the engine running on Government Rd. Officers located a 39year-old female asleep in the driver’s seat. Officers woke the female and observed signs consistent with impairment and administered Standardized Field Sobriety Testing. As a result, the driver was arrested for impaired driving.
The driver was subsequently evaluated by a Drug Recognition Expert (DRE), and a bodily sample was obtained. A search of the vehicle, conducted incident to arrest, resulted in the seizure of the following:
Approximately 70 grams of suspected methamphetamine
A few hundred dime baggies
The driver was released from custody pending further investigation. Officers are exploring charges of Impaired Driving and Possession for the Purpose of Trafficking.
Update to January 19, 2026 media release.
On January 11th at approximately 1530 hrs, officers with the Nelson Police Department conducted a traffic stop in the parking lot of 398 Baker Street, Nelson BC, as part of ongoing impaired-driving enforcement within the community. During the interaction, officers observed signs consistent with impairment. The 29-year-old male driver was arrested for impaired driving and held pending an evaluation by a Drug Recognition Expert (DRE).
Following the arrest, officers conducted a search of the vehicle. The following quantities of suspected controlled substances and currency were located and seized:
583 grams of suspected cocaine
46 grams of suspected methamphetamine
53 pills of suspected Xanax
60 doses of suspected Lysergic Acid Diethylamide (LSD)
5 grams of suspected fentanyl
Over $4000 in Canadian currency
Based on the evidence recovered, the male was chartered and warned for possession of a controlled substance for the purpose of trafficking.
The male was transported to the Nelson Police Department, where he was evaluated by the department’s newest Drug Recognition Expert (DRE). At the conclusion of the evaluation, the DRE determined that the male’s ability to operate a motor vehicle was impaired by a drug. A bodily sample was subsequently obtained pursuant to the investigation.
These investigations reflect the professionalism of our officers and their effective use of specialized training. Their actions helped remove impaired drivers from our roads and prevented a significant quantity of dangerous drugs from reaching the community, directly contributing to public safety,” said Inspector Jason Jewkes.

title: Small Fire Near Power Pole – One Arrest
content:
On
January 24, 2026, at approximately 7:40 a.m.
, Nelson Police Department received multiple 911 calls reporting a small fire near a power pole in the area of
Front Street and Poplar Street
.
Nelson Police and Nelson Fire & Rescue Services attended and located a burnt tree leaning against protective covering on a support wire attached to the pole. A small ground fire was extinguished.
Witnesses on scene were able to identify the suspect, a 40-year-old male, who was subsequently arrested for mischief to property. The damaged property belongs to the local hydro provider, with an estimated loss of
approximately $1,000
.
The individual was released on an
Undertaking
with a later court date.
“Nelson Police would like to thank the witnesses who remained at the scene and assisted, contributing to the arrest of a suspect, who is now facing charges,” said Inspector Jason Jewkes
Report of Youths on Roof – Gone on Arrival
On
January 19, 2026, at 4:22 p.m.
, police received a report of four youths on the roof of a church near
W. Richards Street
.
An officer attended the area; however, the reported individuals were
gone on arrival
. No further police action was required.
“The Nelson Police Department reminds youths and parents that accessing rooftops and elevated structures can be dangerous and encourages safe behaviour to prevent injury,” said CSO Harmandeep SINGH
Arrest Following Shoplifting Complaint
On
January 24, 2026, at approximately 11:55 a.m.
, officers responded to a shoplifting complaint at a local grocery store.
A susp
ect 33-year-old male was located nearby in the area of
Front Street and Poplar Street
, pushing a shopping cart containing the stolen
items. The individual was arrested for
theft under $5,000
. Officers also executed
three outstanding endorsed warrants
from another jurisdiction.
The individual was later released on an
Undertaking with Conditions
, including a condition not to return to the business, and was issued an
Appearance Notice
in relation to the warrants.
The Nelson Police Department thanks the public for their assistance and reminds residents to report suspicious activity immediately.
Calls for Service:
Members of the Nelson Police Department responded to 119 calls for service in the City of Nelson during
this reporting period

title: Media Release
content:
On January 20, 2026, the Nelson Police Department responded to multiple 911 calls reporting glass breaking and yelling in the 900 block of 4th Street.
Police subsequently received information that a male had left the area on foot and was believed to be in possession of a knife. Officers located the male nearby and engaged with him.
During the interaction, the male produced a knife in a threatening manner and made statements indicating distress. Officers maintained distance and engaged the individual using communication and de‑escalation strategies. As a precaution, officers drew their service firearms while continuing efforts to resolve the situation safely.
The male ultimately complied with police direction and dropped the knife. He was taken into custody without injury to the public, the individual, or officers.
Once in custody, police determined the male was experiencing a health‑related crisis. He was transported to Kootenay Lake Hospital to receive appropriate medical care.
The Nelson Police Department would like to thank members of the public for reporting their concerns promptly and for their cooperation during the police response.
As this matter remains under investigation, police will not be providing any further information at this time.

Nelson Police Department – Community Safety Update
Nelson, BC —
Throughout the past week, the Nelson Police Department carried out enhanced foot patrols as part of the ongoing
Community Safety and Targeted Enforcement Program (C‑STEP)
. The initiative focuses on strengthening public safety, supporting vulnerable community members, and maintaining a visible and approachable police presence in key areas of the city.
During the week‑long effort, officers engaged directly with individuals in need, offering support, conducting wellness checks, and helping connect vulnerable people with appropriate services. Members also visited numerous local businesses to listen to concerns, provide safety information, and reinforce partnerships that contribute to a safer and more welcoming community.
Patrols concentrated on high‑traffic and core community areas including
Baker Street, Lakeside Park
, and surrounding neighbourhoods. Officers monitored public spaces, responded to community observations, and worked proactively to deter unwanted activity while enhancing the sense of safety for residents, visitors, and businesses.
C‑STEP remains an important part of the Nelson Police Department’s commitment to community‑based policing. Through regular foot patrols, open communication, and ongoing collaboration with local partners, the program supports a safer, healthier, and more resilient Nelson.
Cold Weather Advisory – Safety Checklist for the Community
With temperatures dropping and winter conditions setting in, the Nelson Police Department is reminding all residents to take precautions to stay safe during the cold weather. Sudden changes in temperature can increase risks for vulnerable individuals, people spending extended time outdoors, and anyone travelling through the community.
Below is a
Cold Weather Safety Checklist
for residents, businesses, and visitors:
✔
Personal Safety
Dress in
layers
, including a wind‑resistant outer layer and warm hat, gloves, and socks.
Limit time outdoors during extreme cold and take frequent
warm‑up breaks
.
Watch for signs of
frostbite
(numbness, pale skin) or
hypothermia
(shivering, confusion).
Keep a cell phone charged in case of emergency.
Check on neighbours, seniors, and vulnerable individuals who may need assistance.
✔
For Vulnerable Community Members
Know where local warming centres or community support services are located.
Seek sheltered areas and avoid sleeping outdoors during freezing conditions.
Ask police officers or outreach workers for help if needed—they can connect individuals with services.
✔
Home & Property Safety
Ensure heating systems are working properly; use space heaters safely and keep them away from flammable materials.
Prevent frozen pipes by keeping indoor areas heated and letting taps drip slightly.
Keep entryways, sidewalks, and steps
clear of snow and ice
to prevent slips.
✔
Vehicle & Travel Safety
Keep vehicles stocked with winter essentials: blankets, warm clothing, flashlight, water, and a small emergency kit.
Clear all snow and ice from windows, mirrors, and lights before driving.
Drive cautiously—roads may be icy, especially in shaded areas and early mornings.
Maintain your fuel tank above half in case you get stuck or delayed.
✔
Community Awareness
Report concerns about people experiencing distress in the cold to authorities.
Businesses and residents are encouraged to keep walkways salted and safe for pedestrians.
Stay informed of weather updates and temperature drops.

Members of the Nelson Police Department responded to 112 calls for service in the City of Nelson during
this reporting period

title: Media release: Nelson Sees Decrease in Drug, Theft and Break and Enter Files
content:
September 7, 2025: The Nelson Police Department is reporting a notable decrease in several key crime categories over the first seven months of 2025, compared to the same period in 2024. This includes a significant reduction in files related to drugs and alcohol, theft, and break and enters.
2025 Stats (Jan 1 - Aug 1)
92 files involving drugs and/or alcohol
151 theft-related files
19 break and enter files
2024 Stats (Jan 1 - Aug 1)
406 files involving drugs and/or alcohol
283 theft-related files
53 break and enter files
The department attributes the decline in incidents to a combination of factors:
Increased police visibility and patrols
Higher staffing levels allow for more proactive enforcement
Continued collaboration with community partners and residents
Some individuals frequently associated with these types of files have left the community or are/ were in custody
“We’re proud of the work our team has done, but we’re equally grateful to the community for being active partners in public safety,” said Inspector Jason Jewkes. “This is a shared success, and we’ll continue working together to keep Nelson safe.”
Calls for Service
The Nelson Police Department responded to 150 calls for service in the City of Nelson during this reporting period (September 1st at 12 am to September 7th at 4pm).

title: First Annual Nelson Police Memorial 5k
content:
Members of the Nelson Police Department are tying up their running shoes and inviting the community to join in the First Annual Nelson Police Memorial 5K Run/Walk taking place on Sunday, September 21 at Lakeside Park. This community event brings together runners, walkers, families, friends and supporters to celebrate the life of Detective Wade Tittemore and Constable Mathieu Nolet, while raising funds for a scholarship in their name, awarded to a deserving LV Rogers student.
The 5K course winds through Lakeside Park, from the Big Orange Bridge to the middle of the dog park and back. It’s a flat course and most sections are paved, ensuring runners and walkers of all levels are able to participate. Strollers are also welcomed. The beautiful waterfront scenery offers a picturesque backdrop for the community to come together in remembrance and honour.
“Wade and Mat had an incredible impact on our community, and this run is a way to celebrate their impact and honour their commitment to the community they cared so deeply about,” says Chief Constable Fisher. “It’s a way for us to show their friends and families, in Nelson and beyond, that their legacies live on.”
Proceeds from the run will support a new scholarship that will be awarded to a graduating LV Rogers student who demonstrates a strong commitment to community service and engagement. The scholarship is being managed by the Osprey Community Foundation, and anyone wishing to donate is encouraged to do so.
Registration is now open online at
https://raceroster.com/.../nelson-police-memorial-5k-run
.
For more information, please email
sperkins@nelsonpolice.ca
